output:
CVE-2024-21797 details a remote command execution (RCE) vulnerability in the adm.cgi set_TR069() functionality of the Wavlink AC3000 device, specifically version M33A8.V5030.210505. This vulnerability allows an attacker to execute arbitrary commands through specially crafted HTTP requests, requiring authentication.

Classified under CWE-74, this issue arises from improper neutralization of special elements that are fed into commands or data structures, potentially leading to unauthorized actions. The MITRE ATT&CK technique T1190, which involves exploitation of public-facing applications, is associated with this vulnerability.

The severity is rated as HIGH, signifying significant potential impact on affected systems. Devices running the specified firmware version of Wavlink WL-WN533A8 are vulnerable, and it is essential to follow recommended mitigation strategies, including updating to secure configurations and applying network segmentation (M1048), and utilizing strong authentication measures (M1037) to minimize risk.
